---
title: "RP 32 Part 1: One Day at a Time"
format: html
---
# RP 32 Part 1: One Day at a Time

Thinking too far ahead can trigger overwhelm ("I can't do this forever"). The one-day-at-a-time approach reduces anxiety by focusing on today's choices. This is not avoidance; it is a proven way to build consistency through manageable commitments.

Daily focus helps you practice realistic control: you cannot control every future risk, but you can control today's plan.

One-day framing works by reducing catastrophic future thinking and increasing present-focused action. Daily commitments become the building blocks of durable long-term recovery.

## Daily Commitment Card
Today I will:
1. Avoid: ______________________
2. Do: _________________________
3. Contact: _____________________
4. Use this coping skill if urges rise: _____________________

::: {.callout-caution}
### When risk is higher

If urges or stress rise quickly, use this sequence early rather than waiting.

Complete this card each morning for one week.

Early action usually prevents the cycle from becoming harder to interrupt.
:::
